strengths: 15+ years of continuous software engineering across diverse domains, meeting the minimum experience threshold
Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work at re:cinq, including MCP code generators and agentic workflow frameworks
Demonstrated entrepreneurial mindset via GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform, founder since 2016) and multiple independent OSS projects
concerns: No Master's or PhD in an AI-related field; AI involvement is limited to ~1 year of tooling-level work with no demonstrated AI products or platforms built at scale
No evidence of director-level IC seniority, strategic customer advisory work, or executive-level influence — all responsibilities central to this role
Cloud experience is practitioner-level (AWS CDK, Kubernetes usage) with no evidence of cloud platform strategy, architecture ownership, or partner advisory
response.justification: The role requires a recognized AI expert operating at director-level IC seniority, capable of advising strategic customers and shaping cloud and AI platform strategy. Against these competencies, the candidate's record falls short on all three primary axes: AI expertise is limited to roughly one year of agentic tooling work at re:cinq with no AI products or platforms shipped at scale (preferred qualification explicitly unmet); seniority is Staff-level engineering with no evidence of enterprise customer advisory, executive stakeholder engagement, or thought leadership beyond niche community workshops; and cloud fluency is tool-consumer rather than platform-strategist depth. The entrepreneurial signal from GISCollective and diverse OSS work is genuine but does not compensate for the absence of AI depth or strategic leadership evidence. The 15+ year tenure meets the minimum bar but is the sole qualifying criterion hit with confidence.
